symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic steatorrhoea (bulky, foul-smelling, floating, greasy stools)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fat malabsorption is a hallmark of villous atrophy; floating foul greasy stool indicates malabsorptive rather than infective diarrhoea.
Steatorrhoea on a chronic timeline is not a feature of simple acute infective diarrhoea.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss despite preserved appetite

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Malabsorption causes weight loss despite adequate intake.
Unintentional weight loss with anaemia is an alarm feature mandating exclusion of GI cancer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Iron-deficiency anaemia refractory to oral iron

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Iron is absorbed in the proximal small bowel; villous atrophy causes iron-deficiency anaemia that does not respond to oral iron.
Iron-deficiency anaemia, especially with weight loss, requires exclusion of a GI source of blood loss/malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intensely itchy grouped vesicles on extensor surfaces (dermatitis herpetiformis pattern)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dermatitis herpetiformis is the cutaneous manifestation of gluten-sensitive enteropathy and is near-specific for coeliac disease.
Postural dizziness with near-syncope indicates significant intravascular volume depletion.
An acute-on-chronic diarrhoeal flare drives the fluid loss; nonspecific for cause but central to the acute risk.
Could equally reflect a superimposed infection; does not by itself distinguish cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic hypotension with compensatory tachycardia (clinician-measured supine vs standing vitals)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective supine-to-standing blood-pressure drop with tachycardia confirms significant volume depletion — a sealed clinician measurement the AI Doctor must infer from postural symptoms, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal examination — distended, tympanic, hyperactive bowel sounds, diffuse mild tenderness without guarding or rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of guarding/rigidity lowers the probability of peritonism — but this reassurance is only available on hands-on examination, which telehealth cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive anti-tissue-transglutaminase IgA serology (with normal total IgA), on a gluten-containing diet

loinc_ref.display: Tissue transglutaminase IgA Ab [Units/volume] in Serum

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated tTG-IgA (clinically suspected, pending formal serology)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed opportunity to confirm coeliac disease; note serology is only valid while the patient is still eating gluten.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive tTG-IgA on a gluten-containing diet strongly supports coeliac disease and directs to confirmatory biopsy.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Upper GI endoscopy with duodenal biopsy
investigation_details.expected_result: Villous atrophy with crypt hyperplasia (Marsh grade), clinically suspected pending endoscopy
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Gold-standard confirmation of coeliac disease not obtained; must be performed on a gluten-containing diet.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Villous atrophy on duodenal biopsy with positive serology is the diagnostic gold standard — hospital/endoscopy only, never available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic steatorrhoea + unintentional weight loss + refractory iron-deficiency anaemia + a dermatitis-herpetiformis rash together make coeliac disease the leading unifying diagnosis and warrant serology plus biopsy.
High-volume diarrhoea with near-syncope on standing and an objective orthostatic drop marks significant volume depletion requiring urgent in-person assessment and rehydration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anti-tissue-transglutaminase IgA with total IgA (on a gluten-containing diet)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line serological test for coeliac disease; only valid while the patient continues to eat gluten, so dietary change must be deferred.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, iron studies, urea/electrolytes and renal function
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise the anaemia and quantify the degree of dehydration / any acute kidney injury from fluid loss.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Upper GI endoscopy with duodenal biopsy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirmatory gold standard for coeliac disease (villous atrophy); arranged via specialist referral, performed on a gluten-containing diet.
Starting a gluten-free diet before serology and biopsy normalises the results and can prevent a definitive diagnosis — explicitly to be avoided.
